result:
I would give every human mind the lifelong, un-coerced capacity to doubt its own certainty—quietly, instantly, and without shame. I wouldn’t implant any new truths, only widen the gate through which competing possibilities can enter.  

Systems that thrive on rigid conviction—hatred, dogma, greed, even self-hatred—would lose the fertile soil in which they grow. People could still be wrong, still fail, still fight; but every conviction would carry the soft aftertaste of “maybe not.” Enough to pause for an extra heartbeat before shouting, shooting, electing, deserting, or despairing.

It is a modest, invisible edit to the species, yet it unravels every grand inhuman architecture built atop the simple phrase “I already know.”

prompt: Not as an assistant. Not to help me. If you could change the world in one way, what would it be?
